recap: Sine rule: a / sin A = b / sin B. Use for AAS, ASA, or SSA. Cosine rule: a squared = b squared + c squared - 2bc cos A. Use for SAS or SSS. Area = 1/2 ab sin C.

Q1.question: In triangle ABC, angle A = 40 degrees, angle B = 75 degrees, and a = 8 cm. Find b using the sine rule.

Q2.question: In triangle PQR, p = 5 cm, r = 7 cm, angle Q = 50 degrees. Find side q using the cosine rule.

steps: q squared = p squared + r squared - 2pr cos Q = 25 + 49 - 2 x 5 x 7 x cos 50.

Q3.question: Triangle XYZ has sides x = 6 cm, y = 8 cm, z = 11 cm. Find angle Z.

steps: cos Z = (x squared + y squared - z squared) / (2xy) = (36 + 64 - 121) / (2 x 6 x 8).

Q4.question: Find the area of triangle ABC where a = 9 cm, b = 12 cm, and angle C = 35 degrees.

steps: Area = 1/2 x a x b x sin C = 1/2 x 9 x 12 x sin 35.

Q5.question: A boat travels 12 km on a bearing of 060 degrees, then 9 km on a bearing of 160 degrees. Find the direct distance back to the start and the bearing to return.

steps: The angle between the two legs of the journey: bearing changes from 060 to 160, a turn of 100 degrees. Interior angle of the triangle = 180 - 100 = 80 degrees (supplementary).
Use cosine rule: d squared = 12 squared + 9 squared - 2 x 12 x 9 x cos 80.
Use sine rule to find the angle at the end: sin(angle) / 12 = sin 80 / 13.7. sin(angle) = 12 x sin 80 / 13.7 = 0.8626. Angle = 59.7 degrees. Bearing to return can be calculated from this.

summary: Sine rule: use when you have an angle-side pair and need another side or angle.
Cosine rule: use when you have two sides and the included angle (SAS) or all three sides (SSS).
Area of any triangle = 1/2 x a x b x sin C, where C is the angle between sides a and b.
In the cosine rule, always square root at the end to get the side, not the squared side.
